project.title: Site-specific glycan analysis of CHO cell expressed epidermal growth factor receptor 2
project.projectDescription: This study provides site-specific profiles of asparagine-linked and serine/threonine-linked glycosylations on ErbB2, a therapeutic target RTK for ErbB2-positive cancer, through structural analysis of recombinant ErbB2 extracellular region and endogenous ErbB2 from ErbB2-positive breast cancer cells. Furthermore, comparison of the N-glycosylated structures of the extracellular regions of ErbB2 and its homologue, the epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR), showed differences in the distribution and density of N-glycans on both molecules, providing new insights into the different activation mechanisms of ErbB2 and EGFR from a glycan perspective.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: The CHO cells stably expressing the extracellular region of human ErbB2 (sErbB2) were selected by continuous culture (~ 2 weeks) in medium containing 600 μg/mL hygromycin B. The culture media containing secreted sErbB2 were collected with centrifugation (6000 g for 1h at 4°C) and filtration. sErbB2 and sEGFR were purified from the collected media by the liquid chromatography AKTA GO system with HisTrap HP for affinity chromatogram, MonoQ 5/50 GL for anion exchange chromatogram, and HiLoad 16/600 Superdex200pg for gel filtration. Purified sErbB2 were reduced with 10 mM dithiothreitol for 40 min at 56°C, followed by alkylation with 20 mM iodoacetamide for 30 min at room temperature in the dark. The reductive alkylated samples were digested with 0.5 µg of sequence grade trypsin/Lys-C for 16 h at 37°C. The resultant peptides were desalted with a styrene divinylbenzene polymer tip column. Flow-through fractions that would contain more hydrophilic peptides, including glycopeptides, were further desalted on graphitized carbon tip columns. The collected eluates were combined and evaporated on a centrifugal evaporator. Finally, the resulting desalted peptides were redissolved with 15 µL of ultrapure water containing 0.1% formic acid.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: he peptide samples were subjected to Orbitrap Q Exactive Plus Spectrometer through an EASY-nLC system equipped with an C18 column (0.075 mm x 125 mm). The peptides were eluted with acetonitrile gradient from 0 to 30% for 135 min at the flow rate of 300 nL/min. All data were acquired in data-dependent mode. The precursor ion scans were performed at a mass resolution of 70,000 at m/z 400 with a maximum injection time of 60 msec. HCD MS/MS scans were acquired with 27% normalized collision energy at a mass resolution of 17,500 at m/z 400 with a maximum injection time of 100 msec.  Acquired data were processed with MaxQuant software 1.6.3.3. The peptide searches referred to amino acid sequence data obtained from UniProt registration numbers P04626 (ErbB2). Up to two trypsin miscleavage were allowed. For fragment ion match, a mass tolerance was set to 20 ppm. Methionine oxidation and acetylation of the protein N-terminus were set as variable modifications and carbamidomethyl on cysteine as a fixed modification. Identification of glycopeptides was performed manually on MaxQuant software, referring to the oxonium ions in the MS/MS spectra (m/z 204.087 for HexNAc, m/z 274.092 for Neu5Ac-H2O, m/z 292.103 for Neu5Ac, m/z 366.140 for Hex+HexNAc). Relative abundance of glycopeptides and the glycosylation rate were estimated from the intensity of precursor ions.

references.referenceLine: Fujitani N, Uehara Y, Ariki S, Hashimoto U, Mukai J, Hasegawa Y, Takahashi M. Site-specific glycosylation analysis of epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (ErbB2): exploring structure and function toward therapeutic targeting. Glycobiology. 2024 34(3):cwad100

Characteristics[Organism]: Human breast cancer cell lines, SKBR-3 and BT474, were obtained from ATCC.
Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: digested with 0.5 μg of sequence grade trypsin/Lys-C (Promega) for 16 h at 37 °C.
Characteristics[ReductionReagent]: reduced with 10 mM dithiothreitol (FUJIFILM Wako Pure Chemical) for 40 min at 56 °C
Characteristics[AlkylationReagent]: alkylation with 20 mM iodoacetamide (FUJIFILM Wako Pure Chemical) for 30 min at room temperature in the dark.
Comment[Instrument]: Orbitrap Q Exactive Plus Spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Comment[Separation]: EASY-nLC system equipped with an C18 column (0.075 mm × 125 mm, Nikkyo Technos, Tokyo, Japan).
Comment[GradientTime]: peptides were eluted with acetonitrile gradient from 0% to 30% for 135 min at the flow rate of 300 nL/min.
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: All data were acquired in data-dependent mode.
Comment[FragmentationMethod]: HCD MS/MS scans were acquired by applying the following parameters: mass resolution of 17,500 at m/z 400, AGC target 5e5, maximum injection time 100 msec, loop count 10, isolation window 1.6 m/z, scan range 200–2,000 (fixed first mass m/z 200.0), 27% normalized collision energy, minimum AGC target 5.00e2, intensity threshold 5.0e3, dynamic exclusion duration 15 s.
Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance]: For analysis by Glyco-Decipher, tolerance to MS1 and MS2 was set to 5 and 20 ppm, respectively.
Comment[FragmentMassTolerance]: For fragment ion match, a mass tolerance was set to 20 ppm.
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages]: Up to two trypsin miscleavage were allowed.
Comment[MS2MassAnalyzer]: Orbitrap Q Exactive Plus Spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Comment[IonizationType]: electrospray ionization mass spectrometry coupled with nanoflow-based liquid chromatography (nLC-ESI MS)
